title: eriopidion strictum
intro: Eriopidion strictum, also known as Mexican Prickly Poppy, is a plant species that belongs to the family Papaveraceae, characterized by its annual or perennial growth habit and its ability to thrive in dry, well-drained soils. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can be used to treat wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Eriopidion strictum include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and provide relief from pain and itching. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into salves, tinctures, and infusions, which can be applied topically to the affected area for faster recovery.
